generations.code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Check if there are duplicates
    from collections import Counter
    cnt = Counter(a)
    has_dup = any(v > 1 for v in cnt.values())
    
    if has_dup:
        print("YES")
        return
    
    # No duplicates: compute permutation parity
    # sorted array is 1,2,...,n
    # permutation p where sorted[p[i]] = a[i], i.e., p[i] = a[i]-1 (0-indexed)
    # Actually: to sort a, element a[i] should go to position a[i]-1
    # So permutation: position i maps to position a[i]-1
    
    visited = [False] * n
    num_cycles = 0
    for i in range(n):
        if not visited[i]:
            num_cycles += 1
            j = i
            while not visited[j]:
                visited[j] = True
                j = a[j] - 1
    
    # Even permutation iff (n - num_cycles) % 2 == 0
    if (n - num_cycles) % 2 == 0:
        print("YES")
    else:
        print("NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    sorted_a = sorted(a)
    
    # Check for duplicates
    has_dup = False
    from collections import Counter
    cnt = Counter(a)
    for v in cnt:
        if cnt[v] > 1:
            has_dup = True
            break
    
    if has_dup:
        print("YES")
        return
    
    # All distinct - check if permutation is even
    # a is a permutation of 1..n
    # The permutation p where p[i] = position of a[i] in sorted array
    # sorted_a[j] = a[i] means we need a[i] to go to position j
    # Actually: perm[i] = j where sorted_a[j] = a[i], i.e., perm[i] = a[i]-1 (0-indexed) since sorted is 1..n
    
    visited = [False] * n
    parity = 0
    for i in range(n):
        if not visited[i]:
            # trace cycle
            cycle_len = 0
            j = i
            while not visited[j]:
                visited[j] = True
                j = a[j] - 1  # 0-indexed target position
                cycle_len += 1
            parity ^= (cycle_len - 1) % 2
    
    print("YES" if parity == 0 else "NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Check for duplicates
    seen = set()
    has_dup = False
    for x in a:
        if x in seen:
            has_dup = True
            break
        seen.add(x)
    
    if has_dup:
        print("YES")
        return
    
    # All distinct, compute permutation parity
    # sorted array gives target positions
    sorted_a = sorted(a)
    pos = {v: i for i, v in enumerate(sorted_a)}
    
    visited = [False] * n
    perm = [pos[x] for x in a]
    
    parity = 0  # 0 = even
    for i in range(n):
        if not visited[i]:
            cycle_len = 0
            j = i
            while not visited[j]:
                visited[j] = True
                j = perm[j]
                cycle_len += 1
            parity += cycle_len - 1
    
    if parity % 2 == 0:
        print("YES")
    else:
        print("NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Check if there are duplicates
    from collections import Counter
    cnt = Counter(a)
    has_dup = any(v > 1 for v in cnt.values())
    
    if has_dup:
        print("YES")
        return
    
    # No duplicates: compute permutation parity
    # sorted array is 1,2,...,n
    # permutation p where sorted[p[i]] = a[i], i.e., p[i] = a[i]-1 (0-indexed)
    # Actually: to sort a, element a[i] should go to position a[i]-1
    # So permutation: position i maps to position a[i]-1
    
    visited = [False] * n
    num_cycles = 0
    for i in range(n):
        if not visited[i]:
            num_cycles += 1
            j = i
            while not visited[j]:
                visited[j] = True
                j = a[j] - 1
    
    # Even permutation iff (n - num_cycles) % 2 == 0
    if (n - num_cycles) % 2 == 0:
        print("YES")
    else:
        print("NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    sorted_a = sorted(a)
    
    # Check for duplicates
    has_dup = False
    from collections import Counter
    cnt = Counter(a)
    for v in cnt:
        if cnt[v] > 1:
            has_dup = True
            break
    
    if has_dup:
        print("YES")
        return
    
    # All distinct - check if permutation is even
    # a is a permutation of 1..n
    # The permutation p where p[i] = position of a[i] in sorted array
    # sorted_a[j] = a[i] means we need a[i] to go to position j
    # Actually: perm[i] = j where sorted_a[j] = a[i], i.e., perm[i] = a[i]-1 (0-indexed) since sorted is 1..n
    
    visited = [False] * n
    parity = 0
    for i in range(n):
        if not visited[i]:
            # trace cycle
            cycle_len = 0
            j = i
            while not visited[j]:
                visited[j] = True
                j = a[j] - 1  # 0-indexed target position
                cycle_len += 1
            parity ^= (cycle_len - 1) % 2
    
    print("YES" if parity == 0 else "NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()

pipelines.final_code:
import sys
input = sys.stdin.readline

def solve():
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    
    # Check for duplicates
    seen = set()
    has_dup = False
    for x in a:
        if x in seen:
            has_dup = True
            break
        seen.add(x)
    
    if has_dup:
        print("YES")
        return
    
    # All distinct, compute permutation parity
    # sorted array gives target positions
    sorted_a = sorted(a)
    pos = {v: i for i, v in enumerate(sorted_a)}
    
    visited = [False] * n
    perm = [pos[x] for x in a]
    
    parity = 0  # 0 = even
    for i in range(n):
        if not visited[i]:
            cycle_len = 0
            j = i
            while not visited[j]:
                visited[j] = True
                j = perm[j]
                cycle_len += 1
            parity += cycle_len - 1
    
    if parity % 2 == 0:
        print("YES")
    else:
        print("NO")

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    solve()
